title: `attrmvcp` will not map attributes to cells starting with `$__`
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Yosys Version: ` Yosys 0.9+3696 (git sha1 c6b5b18a, sccache gcc 5.4.0-6ubuntu1~16.04.12 -fPIC -Os)`

Consider the following yosys script with a heredoc-style Verilog file called `locmap-bad.ys`:

```
read_verilog << EOF
module locmap (
  (* LOC="FOO" *)
  output pin1,
);

  reg foo = 1'b1;

  assign pin1 = 1'b1;
endmodule

(* blackbox *)
module $__FACADE_OUTPAD (input I, output O);
endmodule
EOF
hierarchy -check -top locmap
proc
iopadmap -bits -outpad $__FACADE_OUTPAD I:O
attrmvcp -attr src -attr LOC t:$__FACADE_OUTPAD n:*
select -list t:$__FACADE_OUTPAD
write_json locmap-bad.json
```

I want to move the attributes `LOC` and `src` from port `pin1` to an internal cell `$__FACADE_OUTPAD`. AIUI, the `$` and `$_` are for `yosys` internal cells, and `$__` is reserved for user-internal cells not meant for end use. When I run this script, however, the attributes are not moved, as indicated by a lack of messages after `5. Executing ATTRMVCP pass (move or copy attributes).`.

```
william@xubuntu-dtrain:~/Projects/FPGA/yosys$ ./yosys -QT locmap-bad.ys

-- Executing script file `locmap-bad.ys' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: <<EOF
Parsing Verilog input from `<<EOF' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\locmap'.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\$__FACADE_OUTPAD'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

2. Executing HIERARCHY pass (managing design hierarchy).

2.1. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \locmap

2.2. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \locmap
Removed 0 unused modules.

3. Executing PROC pass (convert processes to netlists).

3.1. Executing PROC_CLEAN pass (remove empty switches from decision trees).
Cleaned up 0 empty switches.

3.2. Executing PROC_RMDEAD pass (remove dead branches from decision trees).
Removed a total of 0 dead cases.

3.3. Executing PROC_PRUNE pass (remove redundant assignments in processes).
Removed 0 redundant assignments.
Promoted 1 assignment to connection.

3.4. Executing PROC_INIT pass (extract init attributes).

3.5. Executing PROC_ARST pass (detect async resets in processes).

3.6. Executing PROC_MUX pass (convert decision trees to multiplexers).
Creating decoders for process `\locmap.$proc$<<EOF:6$1'.

3.7. Executing PROC_DLATCH pass (convert process syncs to latches).
No latch inferred for signal `\locmap.\foo' from process `\locmap.$proc$<<EOF:6$1'.

3.8. Executing PROC_DFF pass (convert process syncs to FFs).

3.9. Executing PROC_CLEAN pass (remove empty switches from decision trees).
Removing empty process `locmap.$proc$<<EOF:6$1'.
Cleaned up 0 empty switches.

4. Executing IOPADMAP pass (mapping inputs/outputs to IO-PAD cells).
Mapping port locmap.pin1 using $__FACADE_OUTPAD.

5. Executing ATTRMVCP pass (move or copy attributes).
locmap/$iopadmap$locmap.pin1

6. Executing JSON backend.
```

## Expected behavior

The `attrmvcp` pass moves the `LOC` and `src` attributes from the `pin1` net to the recently-created `$__FACADE_OUTPAD`. My intent is to then map `$__FACADE_OUTPAD` to an end-user cell.

## Actual behavior

The `attrmvcp` pass does _not_ move the `LOC` and `src` attributes from the `pin1` net to the recently-created `$__FACADE_OUTPAD`, even when I include a `blackbox` model.

## Workaround
Removing the `$__` prefix of `$__FACADE_OUTPAD` _and_ (must do both) including them as a library `blackbox` before `attrmvcp` results in the expected behavior- see section 5. However, these cells are not meant to be used by end users and are meant to be mapped to other cells. Thus I neither want to remove the special `$__` prefix or include them as `blackbox` models. 

`locmap.ys`:

```
read_verilog << EOF
module locmap (
  (* LOC="FOO" *)
  output pin1,
);

  reg foo = 1'b1;

  assign pin1 = 1'b1;
endmodule

(* blackbox *)
module FACADE_OUTPAD (input I, output O);
endmodule
EOF
hierarchy -check -top locmap
proc
iopadmap -bits -outpad FACADE_OUTPAD I:O
attrmvcp -attr src -attr LOC t:FACADE_OUTPAD n:*
write_json locmap.json
```

```
william@xubuntu-dtrain:~/Projects/FPGA/yosys$ ./yosys-QT locmap.ys

-- Executing script file `locmap.ys' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: <<EOF
Parsing Verilog input from `<<EOF' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\locmap'.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\FACADE_OUTPAD'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

...

4. Executing IOPADMAP pass (mapping inputs/outputs to IO-PAD cells).
Mapping port locmap.pin1 using FACADE_OUTPAD.

5. Executing ATTRMVCP pass (move or copy attributes).
Moving attribute LOC="FOO" from locmap.pin1 to locmap.$iopadmap$locmap.pin1.
Moving attribute src="<<EOF:3.10-3.14" from locmap.pin1 to locmap.$iopadmap$locmap.pin1.

6. Executing JSON backend.
```

## Context
Based on studying [`attrmvcp`](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/master/passes/techmap/attrmvcp.cc#L97-L105) source and running `gdb`, `yosys` does not think  `$__FACADE_OUTPAD`'s ports are either inputs or outputs. So if a `blackbox` model is required to set port direction, and the `$__` prefix also stops ports from getting a direction, then this might not be a bug.
